{
  "claim_texts": [
    "AB 31 aims to provide state peace officer status to tribal law enforcement who meet the same California standards as other officers.",
    "AB 31 is designed to address the public safety omissions in underserved tribal lands caused by a decades-old federal law.",
    "The Tribal Police Pilot Program would allow tribal police from three state tribes to gain peace officer status contingent on meeting California requirements.",
    "AB 285 mandates the issuance of a temporary criminal protective order for 180 days when a defendant convicted of domestic violence is released from state prison.",
    "Closing the gap between the expiration of protective orders and the release of an assailant could allow victims to take additional legal steps to prevent new violence.",
    "AB 285 seeks to enhance protective measures for victims of domestic violence and sexual assault upon the release of their convicted assailants.",
    "The implementation of AB 285 could provide additional legal protections and time for victims to secure permanent restraining orders."
